demo.intro: Watch a fee-reminder automation go from a visual canvas to messages on a parent's phone — and the run log that proves it all happened.

widgets.title: Daily Fee Reminder
widgets.subtitle: Scheduled · Every day, 9:00 AM IST · Next run in 14h

messages.text: Remind parents on WhatsApp 3 days before a fee installment is due

steps.title: Every event becomes the next action
steps.text: Vacademy's workflow engine listens to 35+ events — enrollments, payments, missed classes, new leads — and fires the right response automatically. This flow chases unpaid fee installments every morning.

steps.title: Wire it up on a visual canvas
steps.text: Drag from 20 node types — triggers, queries, If/Else branches, loops, delays, WhatsApp, email, push, webhooks — and connect them. Prebuilt lookups like 'fee installments due' mean zero technical setup.

steps.title: Or describe it — AI drafts the flow
steps.text: Type your goal in plain language and AI assembles the whole workflow, asking clarifying questions — which batch? which template? — and explaining every step. Nothing goes live until you review and publish.

steps.title: Test safely before anything goes live
steps.text: Run a safe test with clear warnings before real messages go out. Pre-publish validation flags misconfigured steps, and duplicate-safe delivery ensures no one ever gets the same message twice for one event.

steps.title: 26 automations, ready to switch on
steps.text: No canvas needed — the curated library covers live class alerts, parent updates, enrollment welcomes with login credentials, renewal nudges and admin team alerts. Each recipe is a full workflow you can open and extend later.

steps.title: Fee follow-ups in one click
steps.text: Flip a switch and answer one or two friendly questions — which template, what time, how many days before due. Daily reminders, payment receipts and failed-payment retry links start running immediately.

steps.title: The right message, on every channel
steps.text: At 9:00 AM the run finds Rohan's unpaid ₹12,500 installment and reaches his parent on WhatsApp, email and push — template variables filled from his record, with a secure payment link in each message.

steps.title: Every run, on the record
steps.text: The history tab logs each execution with recipients, status and duration — plus summary cards for runs, success rate and average time. When a parent says 'I never got it', you have the receipt.

steps.title: Drill into any run, node by node
steps.text: Open a run to see the execution flow step by step: what the query found, exactly which messages went out, and what failed and why — like the one bounced email here — so nothing is a black box.
